question_title: Will the interest in “jasmine crockett security guard shot” change between 2026-03-20 and 2026-03-28 according to Google Trends?
summary:
The search term "jasmine crockett security guard shot" spiked to 100 on March 14, 2026 when the story broke about her security guard being killed in a Dallas SWAT standoff. Interest decayed rapidly: 100→57→13→18→4→2→2. As of March 20, the value is 2, having been at that level for 2 consecutive days.

The critical arithmetic: at value 2 with a ±3 threshold, "Doesn't change" covers values 0-5. "Decreases" is mathematically impossible (can't go below 0, which is within the threshold). "Increases" requires a value of 6+ on March 28, which would need a significant new development specifically about this story.

The news cycle has wound down - bodycam footage was released March 17, weapons details March 18, but no new developments in the last 48 hours. The story has been thoroughly covered. While an ongoing investigation could produce new revelations, the very specific search term ("jasmine crockett security guard shot") requires a very specific trigger to revive.

factors.description: Value already at near-baseline (2) for 2 consecutive days after rapid post-spike decay from peak of 100 on Mar 14. Stable tail strongly favors remaining at baseline.
Decreases is mathematically impossible: current value is 2, minimum possible Google Trends value is 0, and the ±3 threshold means any value 0-5 resolves as Doesn't Change.
Historical change stats for this term: 77.3% no-change rate with ±3 threshold, computed over the full date range.
News cycle winding down - most recent stories from Mar 17-18 (bodycam release, weapons details). No new major developments in last 48 hours. Story has been thoroughly covered by major outlets.
Possible catalyst: ongoing investigation could produce new revelations (congressional hearings, additional arrests, formal ethics investigation). Crockett remains politically active.
The search term is very specific - only a development directly about the security guard shooting would drive this exact search. Generic Crockett news won't trigger it.

reasoning:
My forecast:

**Doesn't change: 91% | Increases: 8% | Decreases: 1%**

**Key reasoning:**

1. **Threshold arithmetic dominates**: The starting value on Mar 20 is **2** (near baseline). With a ±3 threshold, "Doesn't change" covers values 0–5, "Decreases" requires a value below -1 (impossible since Google Trends minimum is 0), and "Increases" requires a value of 6+.

2. **Post-spike decay is complete**: The story peaked at 100 on Mar 14 when it broke, then decayed rapidly (100→57→13→18→4→2→2). Interest has been at baseline (2) for 2 consecutive days.

3. **News cycle has wound down**: Major outlets covered the story thoroughly through Mar 17-18 (bodycam release, weapons cache details). No new developments in the last 48 hours.

4. **The narrow search term requires a specific trigger**: "jasmine crockett security guard shot" won't be revived by generic Crockett political news. Only a new development specifically about the shooting/investigation would push it to 6+, which is unlikely but not impossible (hence 8% on Increases).

resolution_criteria: This question resolves “Doesn't change” if the value on the timeline at [https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=jasmine%20crockett%20security%20guard%20shot&date=2026-02-26%202026-03-28](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=jasmine%20crockett%20security%20guard%20shot&date=2026-02-26%202026-03-28) for 2026-03-28 is within 3 of the value at 2026-03-20. It resolves “Increases” if the value is more than 3 greater, and resolves “Decreases” if the value is more than 3 lower.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will access the data on Google Trends using [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/), and compare the height of the timeline for the two aforementioned dates. The specific python query will be structured as follows:
```
from serpapi import GoogleSearch

search = GoogleSearch(params={
  "api_key": API_KEY,
  "engine": "google_trends",
  "data_type": "TIMESERIES",
  "geo": "US",
  "tz": 0,
  "q": "jasmine crockett security guard shot",
  "date": "2026-02-26 2026-03-28",
})
search.get_dict()
```
Note that there may be differences between the results returned by the API and the data appearing on the page. This seems to be due to the 'tz' parameter not having the intended effect in-browser. In this case, the API results will be considered authoritative.
Note that the precision of the timeline will be 1 day, so this will compare the overall interest for the whole day as determined by Google Trends.
Dates are determined in UTC.
If the data is no longer available, or the script fails, this question will be annulled or manually resolved by a moderator.
